user_prompt:
![01-user-prompt-image-01.png](01-user-prompt-image-01.png)

On the company bank accounts page following changes are proposed:

1. Rename Company Bank Fccounts → Internal Company Bank Accounts (header, nav menu title, where applicable)
2. Add account number field
3. Render company name value in company column (now shows company short code)
4. Adjust column order: 1) Company (renders company name), 2) Company Code (renders company short code), 3) Bank name, 4) Account name, 5) Account short code, 6) Account currency, 7) Account number, 8) IBAN, 9) Status, 10) Actions.

![01-user-prompt-image-02.png](01-user-prompt-image-02.png)

On the company bank account form page following changes are proposed:

1. Add new field - Correspondent account
2. Change field order: 1) Company, 2) Bank name, 3) Account name, 4) Short code, 5) Account currency, 6) Account number, 7) Correspondent account number, 8) IBAN, 9) SWIFT, 10) Address, 11) Active flag
3. Instead of having active flag (which just reflects account state), we need a way to indicate several states that can happen to bank account: 1) account termination (with termination date and notes), 2) account suspension / block (with block date and notes), 3) account suspension lifted / block removed.

reference_page.layout_type: AdminPage with table and modal form
patterns_noted: TABLE_CONFIG array with TableColumn<T>[] defining columns with Header and Cell properties
FORM_CONFIG array with FormFieldConfig<T>[] defining form fields with key, label, widget, and validate
FILTER_CONFIG object with filter definitions for search, select dropdowns, and radio buttons
formAutocomplete widget for entity references (company, currency)
Label component with variant (success/danger) for status display
Custom Cell renderer using props.row.original and props.value
toSubmitBody function to transform form data before submission
DEFAULT_FILTER object with initial filter values
DEFAULT_SORT array for initial table sorting

similar_patterns.description: VendorBank page with nearly identical structure, already includes correspondent account fields (corrAccountNumber, corrBankName, corrBankSwift) — primary reference for adding correspondent account to CompanyBank form
BudgetLineCategory page uses FilterRadioButtons for multi-option filtering and BooleanTableCell for status display — reference for multi-state status filter

image_observations: Image 1 (LIST PAGE): Header reads 'Company Bank Accounts'. Columns: Short Code, Account Name, Bank Name, Currency, IBAN, Company, Status, Actions. Status uses Label component (green=Active, red=Inactive). Company column shows company name with ID in parentheses. Filters: search box, Bank Name dropdown, Company ID dropdown, Account Currency dropdown, Status radio buttons (All/Active/Inactive). Pagination shows 20 of 525 records. Actions per row: edit (pencil) and delete icons. Account Number column is absent and must be added.

Image 2 (FORM PAGE): Modal form layout. Fields visible: Short Code, Account Name, Bank Name, Company (autocomplete), Account Currency (autocomplete), Address (textarea), SWIFT, Account Number. Single-column layout within modal. Save/Close buttons at bottom. Active flag shown as checkbox. Correspondent account field is absent and must be added. Field order needs resequencing per requirements.

revisions.overview: The Internal Company Bank Accounts module receives two coordinated updates: a renamed list page with an expanded column set, and a redesigned form page with a richer account status model replacing the simple active/inactive checkbox.
layout_notes: The list page renders as a full content area with a filter bar above the table. The filter bar uses a horizontal row: search input on the left, then three dropdowns (Bank Name, Company, Currency), then status radio buttons flush to the right.
The table has 10 columns. The Account Number and IBAN columns are fixed-width and may truncate with ellipsis on narrow viewports; a tooltip on hover reveals the full value.
The Status badge uses three color variants: green for Active, amber/orange for Suspended, red for Terminated.
The form renders as a modal dialog (centered overlay) with a single-column field layout. Fields are stacked vertically with consistent label-above-input layout.

The Account Status section is visually separated from the banking fields by a section divider line and a bold section label. Radio options are listed vertically. The conditional sub-form (date + notes) slides in below the selected radio option, indented slightly to associate it with the selection.
The conditional sub-form fields appear inline - no page reload or modal swap. When Active is selected, no sub-form is shown.
Save and Cancel buttons are right-aligned at the bottom of the modal footer.

Edit Bank Account

▼

Account Status

revisions.overview: The account status block on the form page is redesigned from a "set current state" picker to an auditable status history log. Valid states: Active, Suspended, Terminated. Current state is derived from the most recent log entry (no entries = Active). Event types: Suspended, Block Removed (implicitly Active), Terminated. Each event has a date and notes. Entries are editable inline. New accounts start Active with no history entries.
layout_notes: The list page is unchanged from Revision 1.
The Account Status History section header shows derived current-status badge on the right.
The history list is a bordered card, one row per event, newest first: event badge + date + notes + edit icon.
Clicking the edit icon expands an inline sub-form inside that row (Event Type, Date, Notes, Save/Cancel). Only one entry editable at a time.
The "+ Add Status Change" dashed button below the list opens an identical inline form to append a new event.
All status history interactions are inline — no nested modals.
Modal footer Save/Cancel apply to account fields only; history entries save independently per entry.
